We propose a single module placement strategy for Swin-Tiny in which one HFW module is applied to the final stage feature map after all hierarchical stages have completed. This design avoids the training instability caused by placing separate Hebbian modules at each stage and achieves the highest test accuracy across all six models (96.2\% at 1-shot; 99.2\% at 5-shot), outperforming its non-Hebbian baseline by $+0.3$ percentage points at 1-shot. We analyze the interaction between Swin's shifted window inductive bias and episode-level Hebbian binding, discuss why per-block placement fails for ViT and DeiT variants in a low-data regime, and situate the results within the wider literature on fast and slow-weight meta-learning.

analysis.tldr: 在视觉Transformer中，将Hebbian快速权重模块放置在Swin-Tiny的最终阶段特征图上，在少样本字符识别任务中取得最佳性能。
analysis.research_motivation: 标准Transformer缺乏快速适应能力，而生物神经系统通过Hebbian可塑性实现快速突触更新，受此启发研究如何将Hebbian快速权重模块有效集成到视觉Transformer中。
analysis.problem: 如何将Hebbian快速权重模块集成到视觉Transformer中，并找到最优的模块放置策略以提升少样本字符识别性能。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要观察到：在Swin-Tiny中，将单个HFW模块放置在最终阶段特征图上避免了多阶段放置导致的训练不稳定，并取得了最高准确率；而在ViT和DeiT中，逐块放置策略在低数据场景下失败。
analysis.method: 提出一种单模块放置策略，将Hebbian快速权重模块应用于Swin-Tiny的最终阶段特征图，在原型网络元学习框架下进行5-way 1-shot和5-way 5-shot分类。
contributions: 首次系统研究了Hebbian快速权重模块在多种视觉Transformer骨干（ViT-Small, DeiT-Small, Swin-Tiny）中的集成效果。
提出了针对Swin-Tiny的最优单模块放置策略，在Omniglot基准上达到最高准确率（1-shot 96.2%，5-shot 99.2%）。
分析了Swin的移位窗口归纳偏置与Hebbian绑定之间的交互，并解释了逐块放置策略在ViT和DeiT中失败的原因。
analysis.experiments: 在Omniglot基准上，使用原型网络元学习框架，对6种模型变体（ViT, DeiT, Swin, ViT-Hebbian, DeiT-Hebbian, Swin-Hebbian）进行5-way 1-shot和5-way 5-shot分类评估。
limitations: 仅在Omniglot数据集上验证，泛化到其他少样本任务或更大规模数据集的能力未知。
仅探索了Swin-Tiny一种架构的最优放置策略，其他架构的通用性未验证。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 在视觉Transformer中，将Hebbian快速权重模块放置在Swin-Tiny的最终阶段特征图上，在少样本字符识别任务中取得最佳性能。

papers.title: Energy-Based Open-Set Active Learning for Object Classification
papers.abstract: Active learning (AL) has emerged as a crucial methodology for minimizing labeling costs in deep learning by selecting the most valuable samples from a pool of unlabeled data for annotation. Traditional AL operates under a closed-set assumption, where all classes in the dataset are known and consistent. However, real-world scenarios often present open-set conditions in which unlabeled data contains both known and unknown classes. In such environments, standard AL techniques struggle. They can mistakenly query samples from unknown categories, leading to inefficient use of annotation budgets. In this paper, we propose a novel dual-stage energy-based framework for open-set AL. Our method employs two specialized energy-based models (EBMs). The first, an energy-based known/unknown separator, filters out samples likely to belong to unknown classes. The second, an energy-based sample scorer, assesses the informativeness of the filtered known samples. Using the energy landscape, our models distinguish between data points from known and unknown classes in the unlabeled pool by assigning lower energy to known samples and higher energy to unknown samples, ensuring that only samples from classes of interest are selected for labeling. By integrating these components, our approach ensures efficient and targeted sample selection, maximizing learning impact in each iteration. Experiments on 2D (CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, TinyImageNet) and 3D (ModelNet40) object classification benchmarks demonstrates that our framework outperforms existing approaches, achieving superior annotation efficiency and classification performance in open-set environments.

analysis.tldr: 提出双阶段能量基开放集主动学习框架，通过能量模型区分已知/未知类并筛选信息样本，提升标注效率。
analysis.research_motivation: 传统主动学习假设封闭集环境，但现实数据常包含未知类，导致标注预算浪费，亟需适应开放集场景的方法。
analysis.problem: 开放集主动学习中，如何从未标注数据中有效筛选已知类样本并评估其信息量，避免对未知类样本的无效标注。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 标准主动学习在开放集下会误选未知类样本，造成标注效率低下。
analysis.method: 双阶段能量基框架：第一阶段用能量模型分离已知/未知类（已知类低能量，未知类高能量），第二阶段用另一能量模型评估已知样本的信息量。
contributions: 提出首个双阶段能量基开放集主动学习框架，结合已知/未知分离与信息量评估。
在2D和3D物体分类基准上验证了方法优于现有技术，标注效率和分类性能显著提升。
analysis.experiments: 在CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、TinyImageNet（2D）和ModelNet40（3D）上评估，框架优于现有方法。
limitations: 能量模型训练可能对超参数敏感。
未讨论未知类样本的再利用或开放类检测的后续处理。
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出双阶段能量基开放集主动学习框架，通过能量模型区分已知/未知类并筛选信息样本，提升标注效率。

papers.title: Dream-Cubed: Controllable Generative Modeling in Minecraft by Training on Billions of Cubes
papers.abstract: We introduce Dream-Cubed, a large-scale dataset of Minecraft worlds at voxel resolution, and a family of models using cubes as powerful compositional units for efficient generation of interactive 3D environments. Dream-Cubed comprises tens of billions of tokens from a carefully curated mixture of procedural biome terrain and high-quality human-authored maps. We use this dataset to conduct the first large-scale study of 3D diffusion models for voxel generation, analyzing discrete and continuous diffusion formulations, data compositions, and architectural design choices. Our models operate directly in the space of blocks, enabling efficient and semantically grounded generation while supporting interactive user workflows such as inpainting and outpainting from user-authored blocks. To quantitatively evaluate our models, we adapt the FID metric to assess semantic differences between real and generated world renderings, and validate generation quality through a human preference study. We release the full dataset, code, and all our pretrained models, which we hope will provide a foundation for future research in efficient generative modeling for structured, interactive 3D environments.

analysis.tldr: Dream-Cubed 提出了一个大规模 Minecraft 体素数据集和基于扩散模型的 3D 环境生成方法，支持交互式编辑。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有的 3D 生成模型在效率、语义可控性和交互性方面存在不足，缺乏大规模结构化 3D 环境的数据集和系统研究。
analysis.problem: 如何利用体素（立方体）作为基本单元，实现高效、语义可控且支持用户交互的 3D 环境生成。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 构建大规模 Minecraft 体素数据集 Dream-Cubed，训练离散和连续扩散模型，直接在块空间操作，支持修补和扩展等交互工作流。
contributions: 发布了包含数百亿 token 的大规模 Minecraft 体素数据集 Dream-Cubed。
首次大规模研究 3D 扩散模型用于体素生成，分析了扩散公式、数据组成和架构设计。
提出基于 FID 的评估指标和人类偏好研究，验证生成质量。
analysis.experiments: 在 Minecraft 体素生成任务上进行了定量评估（FID 指标）和人类偏好研究，但摘要未提供具体数值结果。
limitations: 可能仅适用于 Minecraft 风格的体素环境，泛化到其他 3D 场景需验证。
生成质量可能受限于体素分辨率。
analysis.primary_area_en: generative models
analysis.one_sentence_summary: Dream-Cubed 提出了一个大规模 Minecraft 体素数据集和基于扩散模型的 3D 环境生成方法，支持交互式编辑。

papers.title: Auditing and Controlling AI Agent Actions in Spreadsheets
papers.abstract: Advances in AI agent capabilities have outpaced users' ability to meaningfully oversee their execution. AI agents can perform sophisticated, multi-step knowledge work autonomously from start to finish, yet this process remains effectively inaccessible during execution, often buried within large volumes of intermediate reasoning and outputs: by the time users receive the output, all underlying decisions have already been made without their involvement. This lack of transparency leaves users unable to examine the agent's assumptions, identify errors before they propagate, or redirect execution when it deviates from their intent. The stakes are particularly high in spreadsheet environments, where process and artifact are inseparable. Each decision the agent makes is recorded directly in cells that belong to and reflect on the user. We introduce Pista, a spreadsheet AI agent that decomposes execution into auditable, controllable actions, providing users with visibility into the agent's decision-making process and the capacity to intervene at each step. A formative study (N = 8) and a within-subjects summative evaluation (N = 16) comparing Pista to a baseline agent demonstrated that active participation in execution influenced not only task outcomes but also users' comprehension of the task, their perception of the agent, and their sense of role within the workflow. Users identified their own intent reflected in the agent's actions, detected errors that post-hoc review would have failed to surface, and reported a sense of co-ownership over the resulting output. These findings indicate that meaningful human oversight of AI agents in knowledge work requires not improved post-hoc review mechanisms, but active participation in decisions as they are made.

analysis.tldr: 提出Pista系统，通过将AI代理操作分解为可审计、可控制的步骤，提升用户在电子表格任务中的监督能力。
analysis.research_motivation: AI代理能力提升但用户难以监督其执行过程，缺乏透明度导致错误传播和意图偏离，尤其在电子表格环境中影响更严重。
analysis.problem: 用户无法在AI代理执行过程中有效监督和干预其决策，现有事后审查机制不足。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 用户主动参与执行过程不仅影响任务结果，还提升任务理解、对代理的感知和角色认同，能发现事后审查无法发现的错误。
analysis.method: Pista系统：将AI代理执行分解为可审计、可控制的原子动作，提供决策过程可视化和每步干预能力。
contributions: 提出Pista系统，实现电子表格AI代理的可审计和可控执行
通过用户研究证明主动参与监督优于事后审查机制
analysis.experiments: 形成性研究（N=8）和受试者内总结性评估（N=16），对比Pista与基线代理。
limitations: 样本量较小，可能影响统计效力
仅针对电子表格场景，泛化性待验证
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出Pista系统，通过将AI代理操作分解为可审计、可控制的步骤，提升用户在电子表格任务中的监督能力。

papers.title: Planetary Exploration 3.0: A Roadmap for Software-Defined, Radically Adaptive Space Systems
papers.abstract: The surface and subsurface of worlds beyond Mars remain largely unexplored. Yet these worlds hold keys to fundamental questions in planetary science - from potentially habitable subsurface oceans on icy moons to ancient records preserved in Kuiper Belt objects. NASA's success in Mars exploration was achieved through incrementalism: 22 progressively sophisticated missions over decades. This paradigm, which we call Planetary Exploration 2.0 (PE 2.0), is untenable for the outer Solar System, where cruise times of a decade or more make iterative missions infeasible. We propose Planetary Exploration 3.0 (PE 3.0): a paradigm in which unvisited worlds are explored by a single or a few missions with radically adaptive space systems. A PE 3.0 mission conducts both initial exploratory science and follow-on hypothesis-driven science based on its own in situ data returns, evolving spacecraft capabilities to work resiliently in previously unseen environments. The key enabler of PE 3.0 is software-defined space systems (SDSSs) - systems that can adapt their functions at all levels through software updates. This paper presents findings from a Keck Institute for Space Studies (KISS) workshop on PE 3.0, covering: (1) PE 3.0 systems engineering including science definition, architecture, design methods, and verification & validation; (2) software-defined space system technologies including reconfigurable hardware, multi-functionality, and modularity; (3) onboard intelligence including autonomous science, navigation, controls, and embodied AI; and (4) three PE 3.0 mission concepts: a Neptune/Triton smart flyby, an ocean world explorer, and an Oort cloud reconnaissance mission.

analysis.tldr: 提出行星探索3.0范式，通过软件定义空间系统实现单次任务对太阳系外未知世界的自适应探索。
analysis.research_motivation: 当前火星探索的渐进式范式（PE 2.0）因巡航时间过长（十年以上）而无法适用于外太阳系探索，亟需一种能通过单次或少数任务实现自适应探索的新范式。
analysis.problem: 如何设计一种软件定义、高度自适应的空间系统，使单次任务能够自主完成从初步探索到假设驱动科学研究的全过程，并适应未知环境。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出行星探索3.0（PE 3.0）范式，核心是软件定义空间系统（SDSS），通过可重构硬件、多功能模块、机载智能（自主科学、导航、控制、具身AI）实现任务自适应。
contributions: 提出PE 3.0范式，解决外太阳系探索中渐进式任务不可行的问题
系统总结了软件定义空间系统的关键技术（可重构硬件、多功能性、模块化）和机载智能方法
设计了三个具体任务概念（海王星/海卫一飞越、海洋世界探索、奥尔特云侦察）
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 技术实现复杂度高，可重构硬件和机载智能的可靠性尚未验证
任务概念仍处于概念设计阶段，缺乏实际飞行验证
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出行星探索3.0范式，通过软件定义空间系统实现单次任务对太阳系外未知世界的自适应探索。
